name: Roasted Red Pepper and Chive Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Hold a sweet red pepper over a flame, turning it until evenly charred on all sides.
2. Alternatively, cut the pepper in half and rub with olive oil before placing under the broiler until blackened.
3. Wrap the peppers in plastic wrap and let cool.
4. Scrape off the burned skin and remove seeds and stem from the peppers.
5. In a blender or food processor fitted with a steel blade, combine the roasted peppers, vinegar, and garlic.
6. Process until the mixture is smooth.
7. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil while the machine is running to fully combine the ingredients.
8. Stir in the chopped chives and season with salt and white pepper to taste.
This recipe, featured at Neiman-Marcus, is from caterer George Dolese. George serves this dressing over a salad of fusili pasta, chunks of fresh mozzarella, julienned zucchini, prosciutto, sliced smoked chicken, and blanched asparagus.
